articles.title: Judge rejects SEC’s motion to file an appeal against Ripple ruling
articles.description: District Court Judge Analisa Torres denied the SEC’s motion to file an appeal, claiming that the regulator did not show that there were substantial grounds for difference of opinion.

articles.title: SEC’s Motion to Appeal Loss in Ripple Case Is Denied
articles.description: SEC Chair Gary Gensler, left, and Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse are at the center of the U.S. dispute over the future of crypto oversight. (Kevin Dietsch/Getty and Scott Moore/Shutterstock/CoinDesk)

articles.title: BREAKING: Judge Denies SEC’s Motion to Appeal Ripple (XRP) Decision
articles.description: According to breaking information, the judge in the Ripple case rejected the SEC's decision to appeal the recent Ripple victory. The decision was made by Judge Analisa Torres on October 3, 2023. The SEC's request for a postponement was also denied as moot. The hearing will begin at 9:00 on April 23, 2024. The court […] Continue Reading: BREAKING: Judge Denies SEC’s Motion to Appeal...

articles.title: Cardano stablecoin project gambled away investors’ money before rug: Report
articles.description: Ardana claimed to be creating a stablecoin platform on Cardano, but new evidence suggests it may have lost investors’ money in bad crypto trades.

articles.title: Star Atlas Unlocks Tools to Help Game Devs Build on Solana
articles.description: ATMTA gives Solana developers and creators free access to build using its Star Atlas tools, starting with the SAGE Labs web game.

articles.title: Cardano To Shed Its Gains? Profit-Taking Spikes To High Levels
articles.description: On-chain data shows the profit-taking among Cardano investors has spiked to high levels, a sign that the asset may register a decline in the coming days. Cardano Has Observed High Profit-Taking After The Rally According to data from the on-chain analytics firm Santiment, AAVE, ADA, and CRV are among the altcoins that have recently observed the most extreme profit-taking.
